items.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation
items.summary: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

items.title: See how the price of everything has changed with inflation
items.summary: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

items.title: Back Roads: Navigating bull dust and racing the sun on the Binns Track
items.summary: Journeying through the Binns Track in remote central Australia, Rae Johnston learns why you should always travel with mates.

items.title: Jeff Buckley doco It's Never Over humanises the man beyond the tortured myth
items.summary: The otherworldly singer-guitarist died at 30 and struggled with fame. His guitarist and a new movie honour Buckley's humanity and musical genius.

items.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
items.summary: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

items.title: Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and travel to catch Rinehart jet to Florida
items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

items.title: China inching towards resuming fuel exports as Penny Wong presses Australia's case
items.summary: Chinese state-owned oil companies have agreed to negotiate directly with Australian companies on jet fuel after the foreign minister's visit to China.

items.title: APRA music awards 2026 winners announced, with Amyl and the Sniffers taking top gong
items.summary: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

items.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London
items.summary: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll opens up about mental health struggles in emotional post
items.summary: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll reveals his mental health struggles in an emotional social media post, amid a push from players for the AFL to implement a mental health round.

items.title: High fuel prices hurting regional families taking kids to sporting fixtures
items.summary: Country sporting clubs are rallying to help families attend fixtures as fuel prices double, with some travelling up to 640 kilometres. But it is an extra cost many will take on to maintain the sports are so integral to their communities.

items.title: Threat to homes eases as fire near Uralla in NSW downgraded
items.summary: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

items.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
items.summary: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

items.title: AFL confirms draft changes despite 'disingenuous' opposition from clubs
items.summary: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

items.title: Perth women share experiences of infection and surgery after nail salon visits
items.summary: More Perth women share their experience of needing surgery after having an adverse reaction to nail salon services, triggering a warning from the consumer watchdog.

items.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
items.summary: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

items.title: AI deepfakes are circulating in our schools. What's the right way to handle them?
items.summary: Deepfake content, made from artificial intelligence, is increasingly circulating in schools. But what can parents do if their children are the target?

items.title: What is OPEC? Why is the UAE leaving it?
items.summary: The UAE is leaving an international oil-producers' group that has a major influence on the price of oil around the world.

items.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain
items.summary: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

items.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

items.title: Tigers, Eagles or Cockle Divers — what makes up an Australian Rules football club's name?
items.summary: How have Australia's football clubs come to be named, and what do those names represent? We analysed over 1,500 senior Aussie Rules clubs to find the patterns in how clubs have named themselves.

items.title: Northern Beaches Hospital returns to public ownership after death of Joe Massa
items.summary: The NSW premier says a family's campaigning after their toddler died following treatment at Northern Beaches Hospital helped change public policy.

items.title: Twenty-five-million-year-old platypus fossils with teeth found in outback SA
items.summary: Researchers have discovered ancient platypus fossils in outback South Australia, where they are believed to have lived with lungfish, flamingos and freshwater dolphins. They also had major a difference from the modern species: well-formed teeth.

items.title: Mother who faked son's eye cancer diagnosis to raise money sentenced to over four years' jail
items.summary: A mother who faked her six-year-old son's cancer diagnosis to raise thousands of dollars has been jailed for more than four years for the "cruel, calculated" and "manipulative" crime.

items.title: Ultra-swimmer Andy Donaldson sets world record with 55km Ord River challenge
items.summary: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

items.title: Former FBI director James Comey indicted in probe over seashells photo
items.summary: Former FBI director James Comey has been charged over a social media photo of seashells that officials say constituted a threat against US President Donald Trump.

items.title: Jarome Luai's move to Papua New Guinea makes the power of the Chiefs very real
items.summary: Jarome Luai's decision to sign with Papua New Guinea is a showcase of the economic power behind the Chiefs and marks the start of the greatest rugby league gold rush since Super League.

items.title: US regulator orders review of ABC licence after Donald Trump criticises Jimmy Kimmel
items.summary: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

items.title: Lions star Oscar Allen sidelined for 12-14 weeks with foot injury

items.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs department in voluntary redundancy drive
items.summary: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

items.title: Why WA's unique history has it leading the pack on ADHD medication prescriptions
items.summary: With WA home to seven out of Australia's top-10 highest ADHD prescribing neighbourhoods, experts, psychiatrists and doctors weigh in with their theories.

items.title: Marcus Stoinis hits half-century in Punjab Kings' six-wicket IPL loss to Rajasthan Royals
items.summary: Ricky Ponting's Punjab Kings record a six-wicket loss against Rajasthan Royals in the IPL, despite Marcus Stoinis's breathtaking innings.

items.title: Self-employment can help autistic people avoid workplace barriers
items.summary: After the National Autism Strategy identifies the potential of self-employment to provide meaningful work for people with autism, neurodivergent people who are already self-employed say it can help overcome workplace barriers.

items.title: Early Farrer voters make choices known as pre-polling gets underway in blockbuster by-election
items.summary: Pre-polling commences in the seat of Farrer for the hotly contested by-election triggered by the resignation of former opposition leader Sussan Ley.

items.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
items.summary: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

items.title: Western NSW dam limit increase plan dumped over impact on protected wetlands
items.summary: Water users in part of the Murray-Darling Basin are anxious that a proposal to protect their interests during droughts has been abandoned, as the dry conditions continue to worsen.

items.title: Parents slam 'weak' response at The Friends' School after girls targeted in AI deepfake pornography scandal
items.summary: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

items.title: Queenslanders urged to get tested for viral hepatitis after liver cancer deaths triple since 1980s
items.summary: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

items.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
items.summary: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

items.title: Amount of antibiotic florfenicol used at Tasmanian salmon farm lease concerning, say Greens, expert
items.summary: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

items.title: Australia lacks national strategy to regulate AI spread in workplace, report states
items.summary: Australia risks repeating the mistakes it made with social media unless it moves quickly to regulate the spread of artificial intelligence in the workplace, according to a new report.

items.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
items.summary: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

items.title: Man pleads guilty in Austria to plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
items.summary: A 21-year-old man has pleaded guilty over a jihadist plot to attack a Taylor Swift concert in Austria, which led to three of the star's shows being cancelled in 2024.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball's powerful all-female statement on Anzac Day
items.summary: They both struggle for recognition in male-dominated fields, so Super Netball chose to host an all-female Anzac Day ceremony to commemorate and give visibility to female veterans.

items.title: Big tech hits back at Labor government's Media Bargaining Incentive plan

items.summary: The first police officers to arrive on the scene of a fatal bus crash in north Queensland have told the court of their conversations with the driver in the immediate aftermath of the incident.

items.summary: Multiple locust swarms in South Australia's Riverland region have put farmers and authorities on alert, prompting the South Australian Plague Locust Commissioner to send surveying teams to the region.

items.summary: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

items.title: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop trio The Ronettes, dies aged 80
items.summary: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.

items.summary: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

items.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
items.summary: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

items.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
items.summary: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

items.title: Rebel Wilson describes herself as 'champion of women' as actor gives evidence in defamation trial
items.summary: The Hollywood star is being sued for defamation in the Federal Court by Charlotte MacInnes, who plays the lead actor in Ms Wilson's debut directorial film The Deb.

items.title: Broken PBS system behind failure to list Mounjaro for type 2 diabetics, according to Medicines Australia
items.summary: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

items.title: ASX loses ground, Bank of Japan keeps rates at 0.75pc — as it happened
items.summary: Australia's share market lost ground on Tuesday, and Brent crude oil futures rose back above $US100 a barrel, while the Bank of Japan kept its overnight rate at 0.75pc. Catch up on the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Push to make voluntary assisted dying available through telehealth
items.summary: Despite voluntary assisted dying being legal in Victoria, many regional patients are not getting the same end-of-life care.

items.title: New York mayor announces free FIFA World Cup fan zones as costs continue to escalate
items.summary: New York City will host free events for soccer fans who can't afford the hefty ticket prices for the FIFA World Cup, Mayor Zohran Mamdani says.

items.title: Josh Hazlewood delivers IPL masterclass as Royal Challengers Bengaluru smash Delhi Capitals
items.summary: Australian pace maestro Josh Hazlewood delivers his best performance of the IPL season, taking four wickets to spearhead another RCB win.

items.title: Scenic Rim community divided over proposed Cleanaway waste-to-energy incinerator
items.summary: A proposed waste-to-energy incinerator near Beaudesert divides the Scenic Rim community. Supporters say it could ease Queensland's landfill crisis, while opponents fear health and environmental risks.

items.title: Australian rugby may benefit from copying Crusaders' lateral thinking with Leicester Fainga'anuku
items.summary: Thinking outside the square has previously served Australian rugby well, which is why there are lessons it can take away from at least one of New Zealand's teams in Super Rugby Pacific.
